asset_name: Weight Block Set with Values 1 to 5 (32x32 each)
shared_properties: Every state uses a 32x32 px stone-and-gold block footprint sized for grab movement and torque placement.
Core palette metadata remains primary #C9A84C, background #0D0B14, and accent #D4682A while engraved numeral tones stay inside specs.colors_hex.
The block silhouette keeps flat top and bottom edges so placement on the scale assembly remains visually stable.
differences: This state sheet contains five engraved numeral variants representing weight values 1 through 5 Units.
Heavier-value frames add denser gold inlay and deeper underside shading so mass escalation is readable beyond the numeral alone.
Corner wear increases slightly from value 1 to value 5 to hint at age and mass.

reference_images.label: gdd_markdown
reference_images.purpose: Grounds weight-block dimensions, grab behavior, and variable 1 to 5 Unit mass values.

reference_images.label: moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the weathered gold-and-stone material treatment for mechanical puzzle pieces.

reference_images.label: umbra_character
reference_images.purpose: Grounds Umbra-to-block scale for a clear push and drag posture.

bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load the weight block atlas during FloorLoading and index frames by authored weight value.
runtime_notes: Map one atlas frame to each discrete weight value 1 through 5.
Keep the block pivot centered for stable dragging.
Avoid runtime text overlays on the block so all mass values stay grounded in authored art.

context.usage: Grab-movable weight block sheet used to author variable-mass pieces that alter scale torque.

engraving_notes: All five states preserve the identical outer silhouette.

acceptance_criteria: State sheet is exactly 160x32 px with 5 frames of 32x32 px and 0 px spacing.
Each block value remains distinguishable through both the numeral and the increasing material treatment.
The outer silhouette stays identical across all five frames so mass changes do not alter collision readability.
